title: Issuing Common Stock Above Par
event: Reyes Co. issues 10,000 shares of $1 par common stock for $15 per share cash. Prepare the entry.

entries.label: 150,000 in; par and premium split the credit

lines.label: 10,000 × $15
lines.trap: Market price drives the cash — par is a legal label, not a price.

lines.label: 10,000 × $1 par
lines.why: The stock account holds ONLY par value.
lines.trap: Crediting the full 150,000 here is the classic error — par caps this line.

lines.account: Paid-In Capital in Excess of Par
lines.label: 10,000 × $14
lines.why: Everything above par lands in additional paid-in capital.
lines.trap: Both credits are contributed capital — neither is revenue, ever.

memorize.body: Common Stock = shares × par. PIC in Excess = shares × (issue price − par).
Issuing stock is never revenue and never a gain — a company cannot profit from dealing in its own shares.

questions.prompt: 10,000 shares, $1 par, issued at $15. Amount credited to COMMON STOCK = ?

distractors.feedback: That's total proceeds. The Common Stock account takes only PAR (10,000); the rest is PIC in excess.
You swapped the lines — 140,000 is the EXCESS over par; Common Stock gets the 10,000 par.

accountBank: Paid-In Capital in Excess of Par
build.scaffold: Cash at market, Common Stock at par, PIC catches the difference. The decoy account should never tempt you.
